frozen_components: CoConut and CODI latent reasoning mechanisms
architecture.summary: The system analyzes and intervenes in latent reasoning within LLMs that use continuous-thought paradigms (CoConut, CODI). Latent vectors are injected into reserved prompt positions and fed back as inputs across reasoning steps, avoiding explicit token generation. The paper instruments this mechanism with three probe families (structural alignment via CKA, linear recoverability, lexical tokens) to validate semantic correspondence with explicit CoT, then operationalizes findings into training-free decode-time interventions that steer cached latent states via semantic transport, causal hub editing, and geometric priors (weight-tying projection, energy descent).
trained_components: scalar energy function for latent trajectory monotonicity (Section 3.4)

biblio.title: Unlocking the Black Box of Latent Reasoning: An Interpretability-Guided Approach to Intervention

claims.statement: latent vectors encode compressed, faithful representations of reasoning steps, with early vectors acting as critical causal hubs
latent vectors exhibit high structural similarity to explicit CoT steps as measured by linear Centered Kernel Alignment (CKA)
CoT steps are linearly recoverable from latent surrogates with high cosine similarity
Mapper-Guided Transport improves CODI performance on GSM-Hard by +1.3 percentage points across model scales
Gradient-based Causal Hub Editing achieves the highest gains on standard GSM8K benchmark with +1.8 percentage points for CoConut on Qwen3-8B
Ablation experiments show that early latent vectors are critical; removing all latent vectors causes answer flip rate of 10.7% while keeping only the first vector reduces flip rate to 8.7%
Models with tied embeddings achieve significantly higher accuracy ratios compared to untied variants, with Accuracy Ratio of 2.97 vs. 1.15
Training-free interventions introduce marginal computational overhead during inference due to additional calculation of gradients and projections
